title: Motor Accident Compensation Claim
description: Step-by-step guide to file a motor accident compensation claim with the Motor Accidents Claims Tribunal (MACT).

steps.details: Rush the injured person to the nearest hospital. Under Section 134A of MV Act, hospitals CANNOT refuse treatment to accident victims citing lack of payment. Get all medical records and bills.
File an FIR at the nearest police station or call 100. If the driver fled (hit-and-run), still file the FIR. Request a copy of the FIR and the police investigation report (MVI report).
Collect: accident photographs, vehicle registration details (of the offending vehicle), driver's licence details, insurance policy details, witness statements, CCTV footage if available.

File an application under Section 166/163A of MV Act before the Motor Accidents Claims Tribunal (MACT) in the district where the accident occurred or where the claimant resides. No court fees required.
steps.timeline: No time limit, but file as early as possible

steps.details: The Tribunal will hear both sides. The insurance company of the offending vehicle is usually the party that pays compensation. For hit-and-run cases, compensation is paid by the government.

Compensation is calculated based on: age, income, nature of injuries/death, medical expenses, loss of consortium (for death cases), and future loss of earnings. Minimum: Rs. 5 lakh for death, Rs. 2.5 lakh for permanent disability.
steps.timeline: After tribunal order — payment within 30 days

tips: No court fees required for filing MACT claim
You DON'T need to prove who was at fault for claiming under Section 163A (no-fault liability)
Hospitals cannot refuse treatment to accident victims — this is legally mandated
If the offending vehicle is uninsured, the claim can still be filed against the vehicle owner
For hit-and-run cases, the government compensates — minimum Rs. 50,000 for injury, Rs. 2 lakh for death
Free legal aid is available through DLSA for accident victims
